title: oxytropis campestris var. cusickii
intro: Oxytropis campestris var. cusickii, also known as Cusick's locoweed, is a perennial legume native to the western United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties. In herbal preparations, Oxytropis campestris var. cusickii can be used as a tea or tincture to support immune function and alleviate various health complaints.
